text:
Market today: Inflation expectations cooling (Kalshi traders see June peak) is the real driver - that's bond-friendly and tech-friendly. Semis and AI names should catch a bid if this narrative holds, but CoreWeave/Nebius selling on Meta cloud threat shows AI infrastructure names can gap down fast on competitive news.

Your holdings: SOXL (-10.79%) is the loudest alarm - it's a 3X leveraged ETF that decays daily, and you're already underwater with margin amplifying every drop; stop is $200, don't let it slide there without acting. SPAL (-11.25%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO - tiny stop cushion at $21.38, one bad session ends it. PTNM (-15.25%) is a speculative micro-cap down hard; $9.50 stop is close.

Watch: (1) Whether cooling inflation talk actually moves 10-year yields lower - that's the unlock for SMH/SOXL. (2) Meta's cloud buildout news hitting AI infrastructure names broadly.

Bottom line: Your leveraged losers (SOXL, SPAL, PTNM) are the only thing that matters today - protect margin before hunting new trades.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your hard line - with margin and 3X decay, consider trimming now at $227 rather than waiting for the stop to be tested.
SPAL: $21.38 stop is razor-thin from current $22.32 - set the alert now; one volatile session closes this position automatically, so be ready.
CRWD: Already up 7.88%, scanner shows Grade A long setup with entry $773.96-$774.78, stop $769 - hold and let it work; no reason to add here in premium.
